item.text: I’m going to enumerate matching Rust files using only `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` shell commands, then I’ll cross-check and report a deduplicated path list with an exact count. I’ll start by discovering the available graph commands and query shape.
The direct `orbit.graph.help` tool name isn’t available, so I’m checking the CLI help output to discover the exact `orbit.graph.*` entrypoints exposed in this workspace.

item.aggregated_output:
Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

Usage: orbit tool [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list      List all registered tools
  show      Show detailed information about a tool
  run       Execute a tool
  add       Register an external tool or MCP plugin
  scaffold  Generate a starter external tool plugin
  remove    Remove an external tool
  enable    Enable a disabled tool
  disable   Disable a tool
  doctor    Validate tool health

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

Examples:
  orbit tool scaffold ./plugins/hello_orbit.py --name demo.hello
  orbit tool add ./plugins/hello_orbit.py
  orbit tool show demo.hello

  "source": "pub enum ScopeStrategy {\n    /// Workspace layer only; global is ignored. Used for tasks and job runs.\n    WorkspaceOnly,\n    /// Global provides defaults; workspace entries with matching keys\n    /// override. Used for activities and jobs.\n    MergeByKey,\n    /// Workspace wins if a named entry exists; otherwise fall through to\n    /// global. Used for skills.\n    WorkspaceReplaces,\n    /// Single authoritative layer (global); workspace is ignored. Used for\n    /// the audit trail.\n    GlobalOnly,\n}"
}

item.text: I’m probing argument validation to infer each graph command’s schema (since `tool show` is blocked by read-only DB writes), then I’ll pull source snippets for only the selectors that reference `ScopeStrategy`.

item.aggregated_output:
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "end_line": 74,
      "kind": "leaf",
      "language": "rust",
      "name": "resolve",
      "selector": "symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#resolve:function",
      "source": "pub fn resolve<T, S: ScopedStore<T>>(store: &S, key: &str) -> Result<Option<T>, S::Err> {\n    match store.strategy() {\n        ScopeStrategy::WorkspaceOnly => store.get_workspace(key),\n        ScopeStrategy::GlobalOnly => store.get_global(key),\n        ScopeStrategy::WorkspaceReplaces | ScopeStrategy::MergeByKey => {\n            // Workspace entries with matching keys override global defaults.\n            // Callers that need a full merged listing (e.g. `list_all`) walk\n            // both layers separately — resolve() is for single-key lookups.\n            if let Some(value) = store.get_workspace(key)? {\n                return Ok(Some(value));\n            }\n            store.get_global(key)\n        }\n    }\n}",
      "start_line": 60
    }
  ],
  "knowledge_dir": "/Users/daniel/workspace/repos/orbit/.orbit/knowledge",
  "manifest_generated_at": "2026-04-23T20:40:43.374171+00:00",
  "total_nodes": 1,
  "unresolved_selectors": []
}

item.text: I’ve narrowed candidate symbols via `orbit.graph.refs` and I’m now running a single graph-pack query over those selectors, filtering to entries whose source actually contains `ScopeStrategy::<Variant>` tokens.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'refs_json=$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.refs --input '"'{\"selector\":\"symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#ScopeStrategy:struct\"}'); selectors_json="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$refs_json" | jq -c '"'{selectors:[.code_refs[].selector],summary:false}'); orbit tool run orbit.graph.pack --input \""'$selectors_json" | jq -r '"'.entries[] | select((.source // \"\") | test(\"ScopeStrategy::(WorkspaceOnly|GlobalOnly|MergeByKey|WorkspaceReplaces)\")) | .file'"
